retrieved_docs.title: Preterm Labor -- Evaluation -- Biomarkers for PTL
retrieved_docs.content: Fetal fibronectin:  Fetal fibronectin is an ECM glycoprotein present at the decidual-chorionic interface that gets released into cervicovaginal secretions when the ECM is degraded. ECM degradation can be triggered by numerous causes, including many that also trigger PTL. Notably, a digital cervical exam, blood, or recent intercourse can all lead to false-positive results (which is why this sample is best collected before any other pelvic assessments). [27] The primary utility of checking for fFN in cervicovaginal secretions is its negative predictive value; the absence of fFN is associated with a low risk of birth within 7 days. [28] The risk of sPTB is increased with a positive fFN, though the positive predictive value of this test is still relatively low, limiting its clinical utility in some patients. [17] [29] [30] Conversely, patients with a cervical length between 20 mm and 30 mm and a positive fFN should be considered at high risk of sPTB within the next 7 days and managed accordingly. Qualitative fFN tests are typically considered positive when the fFN concentration is 50 ng/mL or more in cervicovaginal fluid. When a quantitative test is used, higher fFN concentrations are associated with higher rates of sPTB within 14 days. For example, results from a prospective study found the positive predictive value of sPTB increased from 32% to 75% when the concentration of fFN used to define a positive result was increased from 50 ng/mL to 500 ng/mL. [31] Unfortunately, quantitative fFN testing is not always readily available, including in the United States.

retrieved_docs.title: Preterm Labor -- Evaluation
retrieved_docs.content: Beyond the history and examination, the evaluation of patients with threatened PTL includes collecting samples to test patients for ruptured membranes, urogenital infections, and the presence of fetal fibronectin (fFN) in cervicovaginal secretions, as well as transvaginal and limited obstetric ultrasounds. The selective use of fFN testing in patients with an indeterminate cervical length provides the best diagnostic accuracy for sPTL in the early to moderate preterm. However, neither of these tests is as helpful at late preterm gestational ages. The other tests help identify potential underlying etiologies and provide information necessary for appropriate intrapartum care should labor progress. [17] [18]

retrieved_docs.title: Preterm Labor -- Pathophysiology
retrieved_docs.content: Bacteria from the vagina can bind to toll-like receptors on the cervix, placenta, and fetal membranes, inducing an inflammatory response. In addition to enhancing prostaglandin production, interleukin 1β and tumor necrosis factor stimulate matrix metalloproteinases, which degrade the extracellular matrix (ECM) of the cervix and membranes. Additionally, an influx of inflammatory cells into the cervical stroma releases cytokines and prostaglandins that stimulate further structural changes in the cervical collagen and glycosaminoglycans. These changes ultimately lead to cervical dilation and membrane rupture. [15] The degradation of ECM can be assessed by evaluating the cervicovaginal secretions for fetal fibronectin.

retrieved_docs.title: Preterm Labor -- Continuing Education Activity
retrieved_docs.content: Objectives: Identify risk factors for spontaneous preterm labor and underlying etiologies that can trigger the condition. Apply the evaluation of preterm labor, including when to use fetal fibronectin testing and how to interpret the results. Differentiate the management of preterm labor based on the patient's gestational age. Communicate the role of collaboration among interprofessional team members to improve care coordination, leading to earlier recognition of preterm labor and optimal management and outcomes. Access free multiple choice questions on this topic.

retrieved_docs.title: Preterm Labor -- Evaluation -- Biomarkers for PTL
retrieved_docs.content: Other biomarkers for PTL: Positive PAMG-1 and IGFBP-1 tests, typically used to diagnose a rupture of membranes, are also associated with an increased risk of sPTB in patients with intact membranes. A prospective study of over 400 patients compared the use of placental alpha microglobulin-1 to insulin-like growth factor binding protein 1 in patients with PTL, intact membranes, and cervical lengths between 15 mm and 30 mm to determine their accuracy in identifying patients at risk of sPTB within 7 days. [32] This study's results showed that both biomarkers had a negative predictive value of approximately 97%, while the positive predictive value of placental alpha microglobulin-1 was significantly better at 61% compared to the positive predictive value of only 28% for insulin-like growth factor binding protein 1.

retrieved_docs.title: Preterm Labor -- Enhancing Healthcare Team Outcomes
retrieved_docs.content: The primary obstetric clinician (eg, obstetrician, midwife, or family medicine physician) typically leads the care team, assessing the patient's clinical presentation and determining the urgency of necessary interventions. Obstetric sonographers may assist in the evaluation by performing ultrasounds to assess cervical length, fetal weight, fetal position, amniotic fluid levels, and signs of distress, providing critical data to guide decisions. Laboratory technicians contribute by promptly processing essential tests, such as fetal fibronectin, complete blood counts, and infection screenings, which all aid in diagnosis and management. Nurses play a pivotal role in monitoring maternal and fetal vital signs, contraction patterns, and patient symptoms and administering critical medications such as tocolytics and corticosteroids.

retrieved_docs.title: Preterm Labor -- Continuing Education Activity
retrieved_docs.content: Spontaneous preterm labor (sPTL) is characterized by cervical changes triggered by regular uterine contractions occurring between 20 0/7 and 36 6/7 weeks of gestational age (EGA). Diagnostic criteria vary, with the American College of Obstetricians and Gynecologists defining sPTL as cervical dilation of at least 2 cm before 37 weeks EGA, while international guidelines suggest a cutoff of 3 cm. Patients presenting with symptoms such as contractions, pelvic or back pain, vaginal bleeding, or fluid leakage undergo evaluations involving history, pelvic examination, and potentially transvaginal ultrasound or fetal fibronectin testing. Management strategies depend on gestational age and may include antenatal corticosteroids, tocolysis, magnesium sulfate, and group B Streptococcus prophylaxis to improve neonatal outcomes.

retrieved_docs.title: Preterm Labor -- Evaluation -- Basic Laboratory Testing
retrieved_docs.content: Confirming membrane status:  The status of the fetal membranes must be determined, as management differs in patients with ruptured versus intact membranes. If rupture of membranes is not grossly apparent on the speculum exam, routine diagnostic evaluation is warranted. This testing may include microscopy, pH testing, and several commercially available tests that can detect the presence of amniotic fluid proteins, such as placental alpha microglobulin-1 and insulin-like growth factor binding protein 1, in a sample. [19] [20] [21] [22] [23]

retrieved_docs.title: Preterm and Term Prelabor Rupture of Membranes (PPROM and PROM) -- Etiology -- Pathophysiology of Premature Rupture of Membranes
retrieved_docs.content: The fetal membranes play a critical role during gestation, providing mechanical and immunologic protection for the developing fetus. The fetal membranes are made up of 2 layers: the amnion, which is the inner layer, and the chorion, which is the outer membrane connected to the decidual layer of the endometrium. Therefore, the amnion responds to changes within the amniotic cavity, while the chorion plays an important role in immune tolerance at the maternal-fetal interface. [2] The amnion and chorion are connected by the twelfth week of gestation by a strong yet flexible extra-cellular matrix comprised of various collagen types, providing the membranes' structural framework. [2] [3] At the end of gestation, significant membrane remodeling occurs due to programmed activation of matrix metalloproteinases and selective apoptosis, leading to significant weakening of the membranes. [4] Interestingly, tumor necrosis factor α or interleukin 1β induces the same biochemical markers of membrane weakening and apoptosis seen at the end of gestation. [4]

retrieved_docs.title: Normal Labor: Physiology, Evaluation, and Management -- Etiology
retrieved_docs.content: At term, membrane cells appear to increase the production of inflammatory proteins that help initiate labor. [3] A 2024 study found that human fetal membranes secrete a chemoattractant that stimulates leukocyte migration into the membranes approximately 1 to 2 weeks before delivery, leading to their activation. [4] These leukocytes then trigger the release of cytokines and prostaglandins, which promote structural changes in cervical collagen and glycosaminoglycans. For example, interleukin 1β and tumor necrosis factor stimulate matrix metalloproteinases within the fetal membranes, leading to degradation of the extracellular matrix in the cervix and membranes, which facilitates cervical dilation and membrane rupture. [5] Additionally, increased levels of chemokines and cytokines in maternal serum appear to be associated with the onset of labor.

retrieved_docs.title: Preterm Labor -- Pathophysiology
retrieved_docs.content: In patients with intact membranes, abnormalities in the maternal and fetal immunologic response to pregnancy can also lead to sterile inflammation characterized by elevated levels of pro-inflammatory cytokines in the amniotic fluid. [16] Although the underlying pathophysiology differs from the inflammation associated with intraamniotic infections, the molecular pathways ultimately lead to similar clinical outcomes. [8] [16] Additionally, fetal, and to a lesser extent maternal, stress can trigger the release of placental corticotropin-releasing hormone (CRH). In addition to stimulating cortisol production in the pregnant person and fetus, placental CRH also triggers the secretion of prostaglandins from the amniochorion and decidua. [7] Prostaglandins then stimulate uterine contractions and cervical ripening; oxytocin release from the posterior pituitary gland results in coordinated myometrial contractions that cause labor to progress.

retrieved_docs.title: Bishop Score -- Anatomy and Physiology
retrieved_docs.content: Studies in mammals have shown a similar cervical remodeling process, with increased inflammation and reduced collagen cross-linking occurring well before labor. These findings align with studies of human cervical tissue, indicating that remodeling begins before labor onset. [3] Unlike some mammals where systemic progesterone drops before labor, humans may experience a functional withdrawal of progesterone. The Bishop score is used antenatally at term or in women at risk for preterm delivery to assess cervical readiness for labor. Understanding cervical remodeling, including collagen and glycosaminoglycan changes, cytokine production, and white blood cell infiltration, is crucial for managing labor induction. [3] [6]

retrieved_docs.title: Preterm and Term Prelabor Rupture of Membranes (PPROM and PROM) -- Etiology -- Etiology of Premature Rupture of Membranes
retrieved_docs.content: PROM or PPROM may occur for various reasons, including normal physiologic weakening of the membranes at term, increased intraamniotic pressure, intraamniotic infection or inflammation, oxidative stress, and membrane remodeling capacity abnormalities. [1] [2] [5] Inflammation can activate proteases that degrade collagen within the fetal membranes, weakening and predisposing them to rupture. Intrauterine infection is also a common cause of inflammation. However, while intraamniotic infection is seen in a significant proportion of patients with PPROM, it may be either a cause or a result of membrane weakening. For example, while an ascending genital infection from bacterial vaginosis can certainly cause an innate inflammatory response, sterile inflammation from bleeding or tobacco use may also weaken the fetal membranes, allowing for microbial invasion. [2] [6]

retrieved_docs.title: Alpha-Fetoprotein Analysis -- Complications
retrieved_docs.content: Risks associated with phlebotomy include: Phlebitis Abnormal bruising and bleeding in patients with clotting disorders or those taking blood thinners Risks associated with amniocentesis include: Miscarriage Preterm delivery

retrieved_docs.title: Preterm and Term Prelabor Rupture of Membranes (PPROM and PROM) -- Evaluation -- Microscopy
retrieved_docs.content: A sample of amniotic fluid can be smeared directly on a slide and allowed to dry. When examined microscopically, dried amniotic fluid forms a crystalized pattern that appears like branches of a fern tree; this is known as arborization or ferning and confirms ROM. Of note, cervical mucus can also produce a crystallized pattern. However, cervical mucus forms fern-like patterns in thick linear bands secondary to its stringlike macroscopic structure (Spinnbarkeit). This crystallized pattern is visually distinct from amniotic fluid, which tends to form a thinner, more delicate pattern across the entire smear.

retrieved_docs.title: Immunology at the Maternal-Fetal Interface -- Clinical Significance -- Preterm Labor
retrieved_docs.content: Triggers such as infection, stress, or inflammation during pregnancy can prematurely shift the maternal immune state from anti-inflammatory to pro-inflammatory, triggering preterm labor. This process involves increased recruitment of effector CD4+ T cells in decidual tissues, leading to elevated secretion of pro-inflammatory cytokines such as IL-1β and TNF-α. Neutrophil levels are elevated in the decidua of pregnant women with preterm labor and chorioamnionitis. The roles of macrophages, NK cells, and dendritic cells in preterm labor remain under investigation. [19]

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 13 -- Level 4 -- Community 2862
retrieved_docs.content: Early or threatened labor is classified as تهديد بالمخاض المبتسر, indicating a condition in which signs of labor appear prematurely and the situation is identified as threatened preterm labor, a designation that guides clinical monitoring and intervention.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 28 -- Level 3 -- Community 1697
retrieved_docs.content: A caesarean section scar of unknown type and a poor reproductive history are clinically associated with مخاض قبل الأوان (preterm labor), indicating that women who have an indeterminate type of previous cesarean scar or who have experienced suboptimal reproductive outcomes are at increased risk for premature labor. Recognizing these associations enables clinicians to identify high‑risk patients, implement closer surveillance, and consider preventive strategies to reduce the likelihood of preterm delivery.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 9 -- Level 3 -- Community 2316
retrieved_docs.content: A poor reproductive history, reflecting previous obstetric complications or adverse outcomes, is clinically associated with an increased risk of preterm labor (مخاض قبل الأوان). This connection highlights the importance of detailed reproductive assessments in prenatal care to identify women who may be more likely to experience early delivery and to apply targeted monitoring and preventive strategies.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2432
retrieved_docs.content: Cervical shortening, unspecified trimester, is mapped to short cervical length in pregnancy, indicating that the condition of cervical shortening at any stage of gestation is understood as a short cervical length—a key obstetric measurement used to evaluate the risk of preterm birth and guide patient management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 4 -- Community 2849
retrieved_docs.content: Poor reproductive history and elective termination of pregnancy are clinically associated with preterm labor (مخاض قبل الأوان). This relationship indicates that women who have experienced a suboptimal reproductive history or who have undergone an elective termination of pregnancy may be at increased risk for developing preterm labor. Recognizing these associations can help clinicians identify patients who require closer monitoring and targeted interventions to prevent or manage preterm labor, thereby improving maternal and neonatal outcomes.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 2 -- Community 898
retrieved_docs.content: temporal occurrence during the fetal period, providing a detailed framework for understanding how diverse maternal and fetal conditions can impact labor and delivery outcomes and guiding clinicians in assessing and managing these complex scenarios.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 26 -- Level 3 -- Community 2062
retrieved_docs.content: CAESAREAN SECTION SCAR TYPE UNKNOWN is clinically associated with both الانفصال المبكر للمشيمة (early placental separation) and مخاض قبل الأوان (preterm labor). In addition, REPRODUCTIVE HISTORY POOR is clinically associated with مخاض قبل الأوان, indicating that an unknown‑type cesarean section scar and a poor reproductive history are both risk factors for preterm labor, while the unknown‑type scar also predisposes to early placental separation.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 3 -- Community 2298
retrieved_docs.content: Cervical shortening in the third trimester is classified as early or threatened labor, indicating that this cervical change is recognized as a sign of potential onset of labor before term.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 7 -- Level 4 -- Community 2769
retrieved_docs.content: Low transverse cesarean section is clinically associated with تمزق الأغشية الباكر (premature rupture of membranes), indicating that this surgical approach to delivery may be linked to an increased occurrence of early membrane rupture, a factor that clinicians must consider when planning obstetric care and managing potential complications.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 4 -- Level 3 -- Community 2061
retrieved_docs.content: Fetal disorder (اضطراب جنيني), premature rupture of membranes (تمزق الأغشية الباكر), threatened preterm labor (تهديد بالمخاض المبتسر) and gestational diabetes (داء السكري أثناء الحمل) are all classified as pregnancy complications (مضاعفات الحمل). Both gestational diabetes and premature rupture of membranes are clinically associated with the use of a low transverse cesarean section, indicating that patients with these conditions may be more likely to require this specific surgical delivery approach. Recognizing these obstetric conditions as part of the broader category of pregnancy complications helps clinicians anticipate appropriate interventions, such as low transverse cesarean section, and tailor management to improve maternal and fetal outcomes.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 20 -- Level 2 -- Community 763
retrieved_docs.content: and risk factors of pre‑eclampsia, placental disorders, amniotic‑fluid deficiencies, uterine contractile events, and labor‑induction failures with both uncomplicated (normal) and high‑risk obstetric scenarios, including singleton and multiple gestations. Recognizing these interrelationships supports more precise risk stratification, diagnosis, and management of pregnant patients across the full spectrum of obstetric care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 1 -- Community 139
retrieved_docs.content: interconnected concepts demonstrate how abnormal laboratory results, pelvic‑organ pathology, prior surgical scars, anesthetic complications, fetal disorders, and specific obstetric conditions such as polyhydramnios, premature rupture of membranes, and cervical insufficiency are organized within an overarching framework of pregnancy, labor, and postpartum complications, guiding comprehensive risk assessment and targeted maternal‑fetal care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 10 -- Level 3 -- Community 1610
retrieved_docs.content: INFANT PRETERM LOW BIRTH WEIGHT is clinically associated with الولادة القيصرية (Cesarean delivery) and with قصر القامة (short stature). In addition, تهديد بالمخاض المبتسر (threatened preterm labor) is clinically associated with قصر القامة. These relationships indicate that short stature is linked both to infants born preterm with low birth weight and to the condition of threatened preterm labor, while preterm low‑birth‑weight infants are more likely to be delivered by Cesarean section. Recognizing these interconnections can help clinicians anticipate delivery method needs and monitor growth outcomes in infants at risk for or experiencing preterm birth complications.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 2 -- Community 752
retrieved_docs.content: essential for clinicians to anticipate potential delivery complications, decide when normal delivery procedures are feasible, and implement appropriate interventions to optimize maternal and neonatal outcomes.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 12 -- Level 1 -- Community 202
retrieved_docs.content: assessment, early identification of abnormal presentations, cervical pathology, uterine functional disturbances, and multiple‑gestation status, together with avoidance of teratogenic drugs, are essential for individualized management strategies that optimize maternal and neonatal outcomes during labor and delivery.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 12 -- Level 2 -- Community 1059
retrieved_docs.content: health issues are all subsumed under the umbrella of maternal conditions during pregnancy, while fetal distress and abnormal labor forces are positioned within a higher‑order category that reflects the bidirectional impact of fetal complications on maternal care. Recognizing these connections is essential for comprehensive obstetric assessment and for tailoring interventions that address both maternal and fetal health simultaneously.
